response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with strong testing culture and community involvement, but does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~7 months) and a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. High-scale systems exposure exists (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby), but most of the career centers on web/product development in TypeScript and Node.js rather than cloud compute platforms, infrastructure, or hardware-adjacent systems. Formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers is also not evidenced.
strengths: Hands-on high-throughput, low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored testing libraries
Mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, courses, conference talks, dojos)
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience (~1 year total, dated to 2015)
Limited large-scale infrastructure/cloud compute platform experience; career is predominantly web and product engineering
No demonstrated technical-lead role formally mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. hard requirement
Limited cloud/compute platform and distributed-infrastructure depth
Strong testing discipline and mentoring aptitude
